                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     1
MH Scenarios V 1.2                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 1
MH&SM Update                                                                                                                                                                                      3
Scenario                                                                                                                                                                                             3
Scenario                                                                                                                                                                                            13
Scenario                                                                                                                                                                                            44
MH&SM Care Review Summary                                                                                                                                                             65
CPA Scenario 65MH&SM Update

The trigger for creation of an MH&SM Update communication is any change of or addition to the care record information within a care spell delivered by an MH&SM. This enables any or all of the information items defined for use and relating to MH&SM care in its communications to be updated or added to following an entry to MH&SM.

Scenario


After a few days in the Uptown Mental Health Inpatient Unit Tony McMahon (Aged 54) is placed on Clozapine medication along with Lithium and Seroxat. At that time a care plan is also drawn up to help him manage his medication; with a view to eventual discharge. At the same time a care plan component to assist Tony to find better accommodation is also drawn up. Tony is involved in drawing up the care plans and at this time fully agrees to them. Tom Sanderson his Care Coordinator updates his record on the local system. The Medication information is recorded onto PSIS using generic Medication CRE messages and the Care Plans are recorded using an MH&SM Update Communication which is generated at the same time by the Local System

Level
Name
Item/Group
Definition
Update Scenario Information
Update Scenario Information
Representation
Constraints & Dependencies
1.0                  
Patient NHS Number
The currently recorded NHS No for the patient. In the absence of other patient details this enables accurate identification of the patient
12345678
 
UUID
 
4.0
Start: Current Contact status & Location Details
Used to define the current state of contact between the service / professional and the patient
 
 
 
 
4.1
Current contact status
Used to define the current state of contact between the service / professional and the patient
Inpatient (Detained_subject_to_MHA)
 
Coded Values:
Inpatient (Informal),
Inpatient (Subject_to_Court_Order),
Inpatient (Detained_subject_to_MHA),
Inpatient_Absent (Informal),
Inpatient_Absent (Subject_to_court_order)
Inpatient_AWOL_MHA,
Inpatient_On_leave/placement (Informal)
Inpatient_On_leave/placement(subject_to_court_order)
Inpatient_On_leave/placement(subject_to_MHA)
Contact not yet established
Community_Open to Contact (with concern)
Community_Open to contact (without concern)
Community_Contact Lost (with concern)
Community_Contact Lost (without concern)
Closed_Care Spell (with concern)
Closed_Care Spell (without concern)
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
5.0
Start: Dependants
To clarify whether or not there are any persons or animals dependent upon the Patient

Asking the question around dependents is mandatory
 
 
 
 
5.1
Are there any dependants?
Asks the question if there are any people or animals dependent upon the patient?
No
 
Coded  Values:
Yes
No
Unknown
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
7.0
Start: Person with a connection to the care of this patient
This section includes the details for all persons having a connection to the care of this patient including the reason why they are involved - Professional and Non- Professional, Contracted and Non-contracted, employed, voluntary as well as relatives or friends of the patient  - they are identified as SDS registered or Non-SDS Registered .

The exceptions are those identified already as having a specific role and captured by items within PDS - e.g. Next of Kin

Individuals are described in two ways; firstly In their primary/employed role or Relationship, and secondly in any Activity based, Functional or Additional role at item 7.3.1 (Activity based / Functional or Additional Role)
 
 
 
 
7.1
Date Person Added
The date of the entry of this instance of this person
03/03/2004
03/03/2004
Date
 
7.2
Start: Person, Primary Role and Contact Detail Identifiers
 
 
 
 
 
7.2.1
SDS  - Person Identifier
SDS identifier from which local systems can retrieve the persons name, role, contact details and any other information relevant to the employed role they were performing in at the time this message was recorded
(SDS role Identifier)
(SDS role Identifier)
SDS person Identifier
 
 
End: Person, Primary Role and Contact Detail Identifiers
 
 
 
 
 
7.3
Start: Activity based, Functional or Additional role of this person towards the patient
 
 
 
 
 
7.3.1
Activity based / Functional or Additional Role
Defines the activity based, functional or additional role this person plays in the care of the patient

e.g. Care Coordinator - they would be firstly a professional in their own right involved in the care and this would be recorded above - OR - a carer (relative or friend) they would first be a relative or friend and additionally recognised as a carer

NB a person may have a number of activity based, functional or additional roles towards a patient

Please note list is longer than screen may represent - ends with 'Other'
Care Coordinator / Lead Professional/Primary Key Worker,
Other
Coded Values:
Care Coordinator / Lead Professional/Primary Key Worker,
Proxy Care Coordinator / Lead Professional/Primary Key Worker,
SAP Care Coordinator,
CAF Care Coordinator,
Key Worker,
Named Nurse,
Person making Referral / recommending First Contact
Professional making Triage Decision
Professional completing the Service Assessment
Professional making this decision regarding Entry,
Professional completing this Care Plan component,
Professional making Diagnosis,
Professional completing Risk Assessment,
Professional completing the HCR20 Violence Risk Assessment,
Professional completing the Care Review Summary,
Professional completing Outcome Measure or Assessment,
Professional involved in care review,
Non-Professional involved in review,
Person Authorised to Receive MHA Papers,
MHA Registered Medical Officer,
MHA Section 25 Supervisor,
MHA Legal Guardian,
Person requesting MHA Assessment,
Doctor making MHA Recommendation 1,
Doctor making MHA Recommendation 2,
ASW making application for detention under MHA,
Nearest Relative making application for detention under MHA,
MHA Nearest Relative,
Person holding a Power of Attorney,
Person Acting with Parental Responsibility,
Non-MHA Guardian,
Home Help,
Volunteer,
Nominated / Preferred Contact,
Carer,
Other
 
7.3.2
Date Added
The date of this entry of this instance describing the functional role of this person
03/03/2004
03/03/2004
Date
 
7.3.3
Date Applicable Until or Ended
The End of this role or when it  is expected that it will no longer be applicable
 
 
Date
 
7.3.4
Details for 'Other'
Defines the functional or secondary role this person plays in the care of the patient if described at 7.3.1 as 'Other'
 
G.P. At Downton Surgery
Text
Only for use if item 7.3.1 (Activity based / Functional or Additional Role) ='Other'
 
End: Activity based, Functional or Additional role of this person towards the patient
 
 
 
 
 
 
End: Person with a connection to the care of this patient
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
12.0
Start: MH&SM- SS  Care Plan
 
 
 
 
 
12.1
MH&SM Care Plan Summary
Information outlining all the needs or  'problems ' dealt with as components in the subsequent Care Plan as well as any overall comments that require recording (e.g. the items listed in the Example are standard  CPA components)

This helps those accessing the care plan to see the main points without necessarily having to read the whole plan
1. Medication management
2. Accommodation difficulties
3. Lack of structure to day / meaningful occupation
4. Financial difficulties
5. Treatment and monitoring of bipolar disorder
6. Management of drug and alcohol misuse
 
Marked Up Text
 
12.3
Start: Care Plan component 
This provides a structured way to capture specific aspects of the care plan related to a single problem/issue or identified group of problems/issues - It is understood that different HCPs will populate these items in differing ways
 
 
 
Whenever an entry is recorded in this section the person who completed the actual activity must be recorded in section 7.0 (Persons with a connection to the care of this patient) with 7.3.1 (Activity based / Functional or Additional Role) defined as 'Professional completing this Care Plan component'
12.3.1          
Care plans - MH Problem  / issue
The specific problem or issue (or group of issues) that is /are dealt with in this Care Plan component 
Accommodation
Medication Management
Text
 
12.3.2          
Descriptor of Need / problem / issue
Description of the need/issue/problem as determined from either a formal or informal assessment
Tony was living in a Squat before he was admitted, there is a need to support him to find more appropriate accommodation
Tony needs to take medication regularly to treat his mental illness
Marked Up Text
 
12.3.3          
Unmet need
Description any related need that is currently unmet, determined from either a formal or informal assessment
Appropriate Housing
 
Marked Up Text
 
12.3.4          
Aims  and / or Objectives
Describes the aims and / or objectives of this care plan component
To find suitable accommodation - possibly with one of the local housing associations
1 Tony to take medication regularly and safely
2 Tony to be prescribed appropriate amounts of medication that successfully treat his illness and keep side effects to a minimum
Marked Up Text
 
12.3.5          
Interventions & Actions
Describes the interventions / actions to be taken and who / which organisation is responsible for these
Ask Social Worker - Joan Harper to support Tony in this
1 Education regarding need to take medication regularly. /Information about medication used and how to take and store it safely
2 Regular monitoring of efficacy and side effects
3 Regular blood tests for Clozapine prescriptions - blood results sent to Clozapine monitoring service
4 Blood tests for lithium levels
5 Prescriptions for clozapine to be issued by CMHT medical staff following clearance from clozapine monitoring service
6 All other prescriptions to be issued by GP.
7 Liaison with community pharmacist to ensure their understanding of clozapine prescription process and regular supply of medication
8 Regular monitoring of weight, blood pressure and blood sugar levels
9 Education regarding need to maintain a healthy lifestyle, including exercise regime
Marked Up Text
 
12.3.6
GP Responsibility?
This will enable identification of the GP as having a responsibility for the actions contained in this component and to ensure that this is notified to them via TMS
No
Yes
Boolean
Y/N
Default for this would be 'No' unless altered by an entry

A 'Yes' in this field requires a notification of this communication to the patient's GP
12.3.7       
Frequency of Contact
Provided as a separate item to that of review dates because some areas record this in different ways
 
1 Weekly visits from CMHN
2 6 weekly outpatient appointments with Consultant Psychiatrist
3 Attendance at Clozapine clinic for blood tests - variable frequency depending on results
4 Fortnightly attendances at GP surgery to see practice nurse
5 Weekly prescriptions from GP to be collected by Tony from local pharmacy.
Text
 
12.3.8   
Care Plan component Start Date
Commencement of this period of Care Plan component
03/03/2004
03/03/2004
Date
 
12.3.9   
Care Plan Component Planned Review Date
Date for this Care Plan component Review

Normally a maximum of 6 months for Enhanced CPA or 1 year for Standard CPA
01/09/2004
01/09/2004
Date
The date entered here should default to not greater than 1 year from the date in 12.3.7.1 Care Plan Component Start Date unless over- ridden by the Professional
12.3.10   
Care Plan Component Review Status
Shows the status of the review for this Care Plan component
Review Not Reached
Review Not Reached
Coded Values:
Component Reviewed,
Review not reached,
Overdue Review
If the date for this item as shown in 12.3.9 (Care Plan Component Planned Review Date) has been reached and this item does not show 'Component Reviewed' then the value returned should default to 'Overdue Review'
12.3.11 
Summary of this care plan component since last Review (Progress )
Summarises progress for the activities described in this care plan component since the last review
New component - no review
New component - no review
Marked Up Text
 
 
End: Care Plan Component 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
MH&SM Exit

The trigger for creation of an MH&SM Exit communication is the end of a spell of care by an MH&SM. Entry at its earliest point may only determine that an assessment by specialist services is required following a triage decision. At this point, following assessment but without any treatment a patient may be exited from the service with no further MH&SM Specialist Service care. The other use of this communication is where a spell of care within MH&SM Specialist Services takes place and for which care is continued beyond the Service Assessment; this is described below.

Following a review of care, the Care Coordinator / Lead Professional will have recorded in the patient's record the decision to end care by the MH&SM using an MH&SM Care Review Summary communication. The decision to end care and the actual exit may or may not take place at the same time and therefore this communication is only generated at the actual point of discharge / exit from Specialist Services. The transmission of the MH&SM Exit communication formally terminates the spell of care by MH&SM-Specialist Services. It should be noted that this communication should only be generated when all of the conditions regarding exit have been achieved (e.g. that there are no active sections of the MHA still applicable - except MHA Section 117, and that no other activities which may restrict exit, e.g. Non-MHA court or other legal/statutory restrictions, apply). This communication takes place independently of any non-MH&SM generic discharge communications that may be generated at this time; however it should always accompany any generic discharge communications sent to the patient's GP

Scenario

Tony McMahon (Aged 54) was discharged from Uptown Mental Health Inpatient Unit two years ago after making excellent progress. Tony was also discharged from Section 3 of the Mental Health Act 1983 at that time following an MHA Tribunal which gave him a discharge from the Section conditional on his maintaining his adherence to his medication care plan. This has continued to be the case and the Uptown Community Mental Health Team is now ready to end the care spell within MH&SM Specialist Services. At his final CPA Review held a week ago the decision was taken to exit him from MH&SM Specialist Services into the care of his G.P. with the provision that if he shows any sign of relapse the Uptown Community Mental Health Trust will be available to support him. On the day of Tony's exit from care by the MH&SM Specialist Service Andrew Jones his current Care Coordinator completes an Exit summary on the local system. This includes information about the care he has received. It also gives details of all the support activities that will be continuing as well as recommendations for monitoring, aftercare and support. When he has finished recording the Exit information Andrew approves the communication and the system generates an MH&SM Exit communication for publication to PSIS.

Level
Name
Item/Group
Definition
Exit Scenario Information
Exit Scenario Information
Representation
Constraints & Dependencies
1
Patient NHS Number
The currently recorded NHS No for the patient. In the absence of other patient details this enables accurate identification of the patient
12345678
 
UUID
 
2
Start Patient Personal Details
It is important to note that the information in this section has a direct impact upon clinical decision making related to care and risk management beyond its purely administrative usage (hence the need to define further personal information requirements to those within PDS - NB Items delivered by PDS specifications are NOT included in this specification)
 
 
 
 
2.1
Supporting information for use of Aliases or Other Names
Provides supporting reasons to indicate why and in what situations additional names or Aliases are to be / have been used e.g. a child who uses the surname of each parent depending on which one they are staying with at the time
Tony often uses a nickname 'Maca'
 
Marked up Text
 
2.4
Marital or Partnership Status
Information in this section has potentially important clinical implications. The current marital / partnership status of the patient

Unknown at this time is an acceptable answer here
Formally Separated
 
Coded Values:
 Married
 Divorced
 Widowed
 Formally Separated
 Informally Separated
 Engaged
 Single
 Formal Civil Partnership
 Informal Same-Sex Partnership
 Informal Opposite-Sex Partnership
 Not Known
 Not Expressed
 
2.5 
Start: Housing Status details
(Part of CPA required information collection - Need to know this because of potential risks posed by current housing status - i.e. avoid discharge into a situation that may increase risk or relapse.
 
 
 
 
2.5.1
Housing Status
Describes the patient's housing situation - the nature of the ownership,  payment for or service access to accommodation (not the physical type or relationship living arrangements)

Unknown at this time is an acceptable response here
Private_Rented
 
Coded Values:
Homeless,
Care_Home,
Probation_Hostel,
Private_Rented, 
LA_rented,
Owner-occupier,
Squatting,
Not Known
 
2.5.2
Start: Housing Type Information
Information about the Physical accommodation type
 
 
 
 
2.5.2.1
Housing Type
Describes the physical accommodation type: 
House
 
Coded Values:
House,
Bungalow,
Flat,
Mobile home,
Not Known,
Other
 
 
End: Housing Type Information
 
 
 
 
 
2.5.3
Living Arrangements
Further information regarding the living arrangements for this patient

Defines the living arrangement rather than the accommodation circumstances (described elsewhere)
Living on own
 
Coded Values:
Living on own,
Living in a communal setting,
Living with Friend or Acquaintance,
Adult living in Parental home,
Child living in Parental home,
Child living in Non-Parental home,
Living with Spouse or Partner,
Single-Parent living with child(ren),
Carer living with Dependent,
Not Known
 
2.5.5
Relevant Further Accommodation Information
Further information relating to the nature of the accommodation/environment not specifically linked to the property itself - e.g. Has a big dog
Tony has been found a bedsit flat in the Uptown Housing Association development on Smith Street
 
Marked Up Text
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
2.6
Start: Ethnicity / Religion/Philosophy/Culture
Ethnicity and personal belief systems can have a significant effect upon how clinical care is conducted - Some cultures are particularly sensitive about MH issues

Unknown is an acceptable response here
 
 
 
 
2.6.1
Ethnic Group
Formal identity of ethnic group
315237009: White Irish
 
Coded Value:
SnomedCT 372148003: ethnic group
 
2.6.2
Religion / Philosophy
Religious or philosophical belief - if expressed
1041 - Roman Catholic Church
 
Coded Values:
HL7 Vocab for Religious Affiliation

PLUS additionally:
Refused
Not Expressed
Not Asked
 
2.6.3
Supporting Ethnicity / Religion / Philosophy / Culture information
Any considerations or requirements affecting care in relation  to religion / philosophy or culture  e.g. Halal food for Muslims or washing requirements linked to religious preferences
Tony has been attending the Church of the Virgin Mary, Uptown some Sunday mornings
 
Marked Up Text
 
 
End: Ethnicity / Religion/Philosophy/Culture
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
2.8           
Start: Education / Training / Employment and Financial status
Information in this section has potentially important clinical implications. Income and employment status can significantly affect the on- going well-being of an individual
 
 
 
 
2.8.1
Employment / Training Status
Needed to ascertain the viability of any work / training being undertaken by the patient. This is a required part of CPA information collection

The patient may have a number of different Jobs

Unknown is an acceptable response here
224366004: in sheltered work
 
Coded Values:
SnomedCT 302768007: (employment finding)
& all its children - However NOT Including: 365528005: details relating to place of work and employer - finding
 
2.8.3
Education / Training / Employment Details
Provides facility to add additional information about the Education / Training or Employment the patient is currently receiving
A supervisory role has been found for Tony within a Sheltered work environment
Tony has recently completed a local IT beginners course
 
Marked Up Text
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
2.8.5
Start Financial status Information
 
 
 
 
 
2.8.5.1
Overall financial status
Information about the overall financial situation of the individual

Unknown is an acceptable response here
Tony is currently in receipt of Incapacity Benefit, which maybe withdrawn once he commences the employment found for him
 
Marked Up Text
 
2.8.5.2
Start: Benefit or Pension details
 
 
 
 
 
2.8.5.2.1 
Benefit or Pension information
Details of the current benefits or pensions being received by the patient
Incapacity Benefit
 
Marked Up Text
 
2.8.5.2.2 
Period of benefit / pension validity
The start and end of the current period of validity for pension /benefit 
02/04/2004
 
Dates
 
 
End: Benefit or Pension details
 
 
 
 
 
 
End: Financial Status Information
 
 
 
 
 
 
End: Education / Training / Employment and Financial status
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
2.9
Start: Patient Communications and Comprehension
Information in this section has potentially important clinical implications. Often patients with MH problems can find It difficult to communicate for a variety of reasons NB the main language used by the patient is already collected within PDS - this item adds further detail
 
 
 
 
2.9.1
Other languages/forms of communication used
Describes any other spoken languages or formal communication languages used by the patient
GA
 
Coded Value: HL7 vocab HumanLanguage
 
2.9.2
Communication & comprehension Supporting Information
Identifies any perceived support(s) needed relating to ability to communicate e.g. an interpreter for British Sign Language is needed
 
N.B. this is not coded as there is a wide range of observations that may need capturing
Tony needs his hearing aid to hear clearly - a visit from an Audiologist has provided Tony with a new rechargeable hearing aid - although he does still need support to ensure he keeps it recharged and cleaned
 
Marked Up Text
 
 
End: Patient Communications and Comprehension
 
 
 
 
 
 
End: Patient Personal Details
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
3.0 
Start: Referral / First Contact, Triage and Entry
Information regarding Referral / First Contact, Service assessment Triage, DNA / Refusal of service and the Decision to Enter into specialist services
 
 
 
 
3.1
Start: Referral / First Contact
 
 
 
 
 
3.1.1
Date Referral received / First Contact made:
Date when referral received by the Specialist Service or when First Contact was made
01/01/2004
 
Date
 
3.1.2
Is this a self referral by this Patient?
Has the patient self-referred - if NO then this prompts for recording the person making the referral
No
 
Boolean (Yes/No)
Whenever an entry is recorded in this section the person who completed the actual activity must be recorded in section 7.0 (Persons with a connection to the care of this patient) with 7.3.1 (Activity based / Functional or Additional Role) defined as 'Person making Referral / recommending First Contact'
3.1.3
Reason for Referral / First contact
The reason given for the referral by the person making it - or as collected by the MH&SM professional if this is a first contact without formal referral
Tony was expressing some grandiose ideas about who he is that lead staff at the hostel to become concerned for him. He can be aggressive and personally invasive, however he had not been observed being physically violent.
 
Marked Up Text
 
 
End: Referral / First Contact
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
3.2
Start: Triage Decision
May also be used if this is a reactivation of activity when someone presents at another service following Absconding or needs a new service assessment whilst in a spell of care (i.e. an Entry communication may already exists)
 
 
 
Whenever an entry is recorded in this section the person who completed the actual activity must be recorded in section 7.0 (Persons with a connection to the care of this patient) with 7.3.1 (Activity based / Functional or Additional Role) defined as 'Professional making Triage Decision'
3.2.1
Triage Decision
Triage decision regarding urgency of Service Assessment following first contact / referral to service
Urgent
 
Coded Values:
Emergency,
Serious (CAMHS),
Urgent,
Routine
'Serious' is only for use by services identified as delivering CAMHS
3.2.2
Supporting Triage Information
Supporting information regarding the Triage Decision
The referral indicates that it is important that Tony is seen urgently
 
Marked Up Text
 
3.2.3
Date Triage completed
Date when the triage decision was completed
01/01/2004
 
Date
 
 
End: Triage Decision
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
3.4
Start: Specialist Service Assessment
 
 
 
 
Whenever an entry is recorded in this section the person who completed the actual activity must be recorded in section 7.0 (Persons with a connection to the care of this patient) with 7.3.1 (Activity based / Functional or Additional Role) defined as 'Professional completing the Service Assessment'
3.4.1
Date of Specialist Service Assessment
Captures the date of the assessment, particularly if different from actual entry to service
01/01/2004
 
Date
 
3.4.2
Presenting Problem(s)
Details of any presenting problem(s) or issue(s)
Tony was expressing grandiose ideas and was aggressive and invasive
 
Marked Up Text
 
3.4.3
Outcome of Specialist Service Assessment
Statement regarding the outcome of the Specialist Service Assessment

NB the outcome could be no further action by this service with or without recommendations for care elsewhere
Tony's symptoms indicate hypomania. Difficulties with accommodation were also identified.
 
Marked Up Text
 
 
End: Specialist Service Assessment
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
3.5
Start: Entry to MH&SM Specialist Services
For capturing the information around the Entry to Specialist Services
 
 
 
Whenever an entry is recorded in this section the person who completed the actual activity must be recorded in section 7.0 (Persons with a connection to the care of this patient) with 7.3.1 (Activity based / Functional or Additional Role) defined as 'Professional making this decision regarding Entry'
3.5.1
Date of Entry or Re-Entry to MH&SM-SS
Date when decision was taken to Enter patient into MH&SM-SS - or re-enter in the case of patients re- presenting following an absence from service where the spell has not been closed by an Exit - e.g. Patient absconded some time ago
01/01/2004
 
Date
 
3.5.2
Summary of decision taken
Summarises decision including reasons FOR or for NOT Entering this Patient into MH&SM-SS 

NB An Entry may take place following a Triage decision, Service Assessment, without a service assessment following detention following an MHA Assessments or for some Court Order based admissions
The decision was taken to admit Tony for assessment and treatment
 
Marked Up Text
 
 
End: Entry to MH&SM Specialist Services
 
 
 
 
 
 
End: Referral / First Contact, Triage and Entry
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
4
Start: Current Contact status & Location Details
Used to define the current state of contact between the service / professional and the patient
 
 
 
 
4.1
Current contact status
Used to define the current state of contact between the service / professional and the patient
Community Open to Contact (without concern)

Inpatient (Informal),
Inpatient (Subject_to_Court_Order),

Closed_Care Spell (with concern)
Closed_Care Spell (without concern)
 
4.3
Information regarding Current Location of Patient
Enables the recording of information to support where the patient is currently located - may be necessary for temporary placements or where a unit is spread out across a campus

Another example of its usage is where a child is placed with temporary foster carers but still retains parental home address as current address within PDS
Home address
 
Marked Up Text
 
4.4
Current Location and Contact Status Address
Address of the current location - would be an SDS reference for SDS registered location
 
HL7 Unstructured Address

5
Start: Dependants
To clarify whether or not there are any persons or animals dependent upon the Patient

Asking the question around dependents is mandatory
 
 
 
 
5.1
Are there any dependants?
Asks the question if there are any people or animals dependent upon the patient?
No
 
Coded  Values:
Yes
No
Unknown
 
 
End: Dependants
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
6.0
Start: Care Teams or organisations involved in care delivery
Enables capture of all the SDS and non-SDS registered care teams and disciplines caring for this patient at this time as well as inputs from external organisations

e.g. Care being delivered by a voluntary agent under contract from the NHS - residential care homes etc.
 
 
 
 
6.1
Main or Additional Care Team Definition
There can only be one Main Care Team at any point in care - this would usually be the team from which the Lead Professional or Care Coordinator works (however this is not always the case)

NB This item captures the nature of the involvement of the organisation and not the organisation itself
Main
 
Coded Value:
Main,
Additional
There can only be one Care Team currently described as 'Main' at any time
6.2
Start: SDS Registered Care Teams
 
 
 
 
 
6.2.1
Responsible Care Team
SDS Work Group Identifier and details  - there may be more than one unit / care team actively caring for a patient at any single point
(SDS Organisation Identifier)
 
SDS Organisation identifier
 
6.2.2
Date care by this team commenced
Date when the first entry for this team was recorded
04/05/2004
 
Date
 
 
End: SDS Registered Care Teams
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
6.4
Start: Care Activity or Additional Role / Function provided by this organisation
This enables capture of any formal or other role that this organisation may be taking on in respect ot this patient.

Organisations can take on a number of formal roles e.g. MHA Guardianship where there is no suitable family member available for these roles
 
 
 
 
6.4.1
Description of involvement in patient care or support
Describes an aspect of the involvement this organisation has in the care or support of this patient
Uptown Social Services
 
Text
 
6.4.2
Legal or statutory Role / Responsibility undertaken by this organisation
Any Legal or Statutory responsibilities undertaken by this orgainisation

Defined in this way to enable systems to make clear identification of specific roles
 Organisation with MHA Section 117 responsibility
 
Coded Values:
Organisation with MHA Legal Guardianship responsibility,
Organisation acting as MHA Nearest Relative,
Organisation holding a Power of Attorney,
Organisation with Parental Responsibility,
Organisation with MHA Section 117 responsibility
Organisation funding care
Other
There can only be one entry in the record against the folloiwng items from within this list:
- Organisation acting as MHA Legal Guardian,
- Organisation acting as MHA Nearest Relative,
- Organisation holding a Power of Attorney,
- Organisation with Parental Responsibility,
- Organisation with MHA Section 117 responsibility
6.4.3
Definition of legal / statutory role of this organisation if 'Other'
Defines the role of the organisation if it is described at 6.4.2 as 'Other'
 
 
Text
If 6.4.2 = 'Other' this item must be available otherwise it must NOT be available
 
End: Care Activity or Additional Role / Function provided by this organisation
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
6.5
Start: Other Organisation Service User Identifiers
This provides opportunity to capture any unique identifiers / case numbers etc.that relate specifically to the patient (Service User) and which might be of use when dealing with the above organisation(s) - Important for inter-agency working, enabling tracing and reduction of risk factors

e.g. National Insurance Number
 
 
 
 
6.5.1
Identifier type
Supports external organisations to identify this person and describes what type of identifier it is e.g. National Insurance number, Social Care unique identifier, Passport no etc
National Insurance No
 
Text
 
6.5.2
Other Unique Identifier
Supports external organisations to identify this person e.g. National Insurance number, Social Care unique identifier, Passport no etc
AA 01 01 01 A
 
Text
 
 
End: Other Organisation Service User Identifiers
 
 
 
 
 
 
End: Care Teams or organisations involved in care delivery
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
7
Start: Person with a connection to the care of this patient
This section includes the details for all persons having a connection to the care of this patient including the reason why they are involved - Professional and Non- Professional, Contracted and Non-contracted, employed, voluntary as well as relatives or friends of the patient  - they are identified as SDS registered or Non-SDS Registered .

The exceptions are those identified already as having a specific role and captured by items within PDS - e.g. Next of Kin

Individuals are described in two ways; firstly In their primary/employed role or Relationship, and secondly in any Activity based, Functional or Additional role at item 7.3.1 (Activity based / Functional or Additional Role)
 
 
 
 
7.1
Date Person Added
The date of the entry of this instance of this person
03/03/2004
03/03/2004
Date
 
7.2
Start: Person, Primary Role and Contact Detail Identifiers
 
 
 
 
 
7.2.1
SDS  - Person Identifier
SDS identifier from which local systems can retrieve the persons name, role, contact details and any other information relevant to the employed role they were performing in at the time this message was recorded
(SDS role Identifier)
(SDS role Identifier)
SDS person Identifier
 
 
End: Person, Primary Role and Contact Detail Identifiers
 
 
 
 
 
7.3
Start: Activity based, Functional or Additional role of this person towards the patient
 
 
 
 
 
7.3.1
Activity based / Functional or Additional Role
Defines the activity based, functional or additional role this person plays in the care of the patient

e.g. Care Coordinator - they would be firstly a professional in their own right involved in the care and this would be recorded above - OR - a carer (relative or friend) they would first be a relative or friend and additionally recognised as a carer

NB a person may have a number of activity based, functional or additional roles towards a patient

Please note list is longer than screen may represent - ends with 'Other'
Care Coordinator / Lead Professional/Primary Key Worker,
Other
Coded Values:
Care Coordinator / Lead Professional/Primary Key Worker,
Proxy Care Coordinator / Lead Professional/Primary Key Worker,
SAP Care Coordinator,
CAF Care Coordinator,
Key Worker,
Named Nurse,
Person making Referral / recommending First Contact
Professional making Triage Decision
Professional completing the Service Assessment
Professional making this decision regarding Entry,
Professional completing this Care Plan component,
Professional making Diagnosis,
Professional completing Risk Assessment,
Professional completing the HCR20 Violence Risk Assessment,
Professional completing the Care Review Summary,
Professional completing Outcome Measure or Assessment,
Professional involved in care review,
Non-Professional involved in review,
Person Authorised to Receive MHA Papers,
MHA Registered Medical Officer,
MHA Section 25 Supervisor,
MHA Legal Guardian,
Person requesting MHA Assessment,
Doctor making MHA Recommendation 1,
Doctor making MHA Recommendation 2,
ASW making application for detention under MHA,
Nearest Relative making application for detention under MHA,
MHA Nearest Relative,
Person holding a Power of Attorney,
Person Acting with Parental Responsibility,
Non-MHA Guardian,
Home Help,
Volunteer,
Nominated / Preferred Contact,
Carer,
Other
 
7.3.2
Date Added
The date of this entry of this instance describing the functional role of this person
03/03/2004
03/03/2004
Date
 
7.3.3
Date Applicable Until or Ended
The End of this role or when it  is expected that it will no longer be applicable
 
 
Date
 
7.3.4
Details for 'Other'
Defines the functional or secondary role this person plays in the care of the patient if described at 7.3.1 as 'Other'
 
G.P. At Downton Surgery
Text
Only for use if item 7.3.1 (Activity based / Functional or Additional Role) ='Other'
 
End: Activity based, Functional or Additional role of this person towards the patient
 
 
 
 
 
 
End: Person with a connection to the care of this patient
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
8.0
Start: Care Process and Review
Items relating to the administration of reviews of care for CPA, MOC, UnSpecified
 
 
 
 
8.1
Start: Care Process Administration
 
 
 
 
 
8.1.1
Care Process being used
Which care process is this review being delivered under at this time
CPA
 
Coded Values:
CPA, MOC, UnSpecified
 
8.1.2
Care Level
Defines the current level of care planning or approach being taken
Enhanced
 
Coded Values: Single_level, Standard, Enhanced
 
8.1.3
Association with other Care Processes
Shows whether this Care Review Summary is part of or is to be read in conjunction with an external care process e.g. SAP, CAF
 
 
Text
 
8.1.4
Date Care Process commenced or First Review
Enables HSCPs to identify the span of CPA / MOC / Unspecified review activities within this spell of care
01/01/2004
 
Date
 
8.1.5
Date Care Process concluded or Most Recent Review
To enable the identification of the end of CPA / MOC / Unspecified activities or when the most recent review took place
10/09/2006
 
Date
 
 
End: Care Process Administration
 
 
 
 
 
 
End: Care Process and Review
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
9
Start: Diagnosis/es and Registered Disabilities/Other Conditions
Diagnosis/es to be recorded PSIS
 
 
 
 
9.1
Start: Diagnosis Coding
Snomed CT and ICD-10 (but only where a mapped Snomed CT Code exists)codes for diagnoses
 
 
 
Whenever an entry is recorded in this section the person who completed the actual activity must be recorded in section 7.0 (Persons with a connection to the care of this patient) with 7.3.1 (Activity based / Functional or Additional Role) defined as 'Professional making Diagnosis'
9.1.1
Diagnosis 
Diagnostic coding - completed by HSCP using longhand descriptor to generate code - receiving system will render longhand diagnosis from code
231496004: hypomania
 
Coded Values:
Snomed CT
15611000000104 Diagnosis
 
9.1.2
Mental Health ICD-10 Representation
ICD-10 is a list of codes widely used for diagnostic purposes within MH&SM-SSs - Can exist as coded item as long as a Snomed code with a direct relationship to the ICD-10 code used is also present 
F30.1
 
Coded Values:
ICD-10
An ICD-10 code here would be generated only following an equivalent Snomed CT code being entered at 9.1.1 (Diagnosis) i.e. This item can only be included if it has a directly mapped relationship to 9.1.1 (Diagnosis)
9.1.3 
Date of Diagnosis 
Date the Diagnosis was formally recorded
12/02/2004
 
Date
 
 
End: Diagnosis Coding
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
9.2
Start: Registered Disability / Other condition(s)
Conditions or Disabilities that may or may not currently be formally captured as diagnosis/es - these will usually form the basis of observations by HSCPs and could include minor conditions for which the patient is self-treating as well as treatments or supports needed to enable the patient to be cared for
 
 
 
 
9.2.1
Disability or condition not otherwise mentioned
These will usually form the basis of observations by HSCPs as well as registered Disabilities for which there may or may not be current treatment underway
Tony has a hearing problem for which he has seen an Audiologist and has been fitted with appropriate new hearing aids
 
Text
 
9.2.2
Care / Treatment or Support required
Description of the care, Treatment or support needed for this Condition
Tony has seen Audiologist
 
Marked Up Text
 
9.2.3
Is this a Registered Disability?
Defines if this item is a disability for which the patient is formally Registered as Disabled
No
 
Boolean:
Yes/No
 
 
End: Registered Disability / Other condition(s)
 
 
 
 
 
 
End: Diagnosis/es and Registered Disabilities/Other Conditions
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
10.0
Start: Substance Misuse Information
Specific information to support immediate care for Substance Misuse
 
 
 
 
10.1
Start: Problem Substances Information
Details around the substance(s) that are known to be / have been misused by this person
 
 
 
 
10.1.1
Order of usage of substance
The Order of priority of usage of the substance by the patient
1
 
Integer
 
10.1.2
Problem Substance
Description of the substance being misused
Alchohol
 
Text
 
10.1.3
Age of first use
Age at which this substance was first known to be misused
12 years
 
Text
 
10.1.4
Route of administration
Which route this substance is used by the patient
Orally
 
Text
 
 
End: Problem Substances Information
 
 
 
 
 
10.2
Injecting Status
Is the client currently injecting, have they ever previously injected or never injected?
Never Injected
 
Coded Value:
Currently Injecting
Previously Injected,
Never Injected,
Not Known
 
 
End: Substance Misuse Information
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
11.0
Start: Legal Activities 
 
 
 
 
 
11.2
Is Patient currently subject to MHA '83?
Checks whether the patient is subject to MHA '83 (Excluding MHA Section 117)
No
 
Boolean
(Yes/No)
Excludes MHA section 117

A “Yes” answer may only be given when the MHA papers have been accepted by the person authorised to receive them
11.3.3
MHA History
For rendering an accumulative MHA history which HSCP may add to it in that session
Detained under MHA Section 2 on 01/01/2004 with symptoms of Hypomania.
This was converted to a section 3 on 26/01/2004.
Section 3 was renewed on 24 /04/2004.
Tony was removed from Section on 09/09/04.

 
Marked Up Text
This item can only be able to be added to over time - any current entry/ies can only add to the content of this item not replace it
 
End: Mental Health Act 1983 Activities
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
11.5
Start: Capacity to Consent
To define the current capacity to consent to care or treatment as defined in the Mental Capacity Act 2005 - particularly relevant around Children's care and Fraser competencies (formerly 'Gillick')

NB it is important to state with this item that capacity to consent issues are not necessarily global to all conditions or treatments
 
 
 
 
11.5.1
Aspect of Care this item relates to
Defines the specific aspect of care this item relates to
 
Text
 
11.5.2
Capacity to Consent
Defines the current state of capacity to consent and who should be contacted in the event that a consent activity related to this aspect of care is required
 
Marked Up Text
 
11.5.3
Date recorded
Date when capacity issue recorded
 
Date
 
 
End: Capacity to Consent
 
 
 
 
 
 
End: Legal Activities 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
12.0
Start: MH&SM- SS  Care Plan
 
 
 
 
 
12.1
MH&SM Care Plan Summary
Information outlining all the needs or  'problems ' dealt with as components in the subsequent Care Plan as well as any overall comments that require recording (e.g. the items listed in the Example are standard  CPA components)

This helps those accessing the care plan to see the main points without necessarily having to read the whole plan
CPA has ended and there are no continuing care plan components at this time
 
Marked Up Text
 
12.2
Diary of planned activities
Important for defining structured plan for Service Users daily activities
Monday am - Mind Sheltered Workshop - continuing
Tuesday am - Rethink Drop-In - continuing
Wednesday pm - Mind Sheltered Workshop - continuing
Friday am - Mind Sheltered Workshop - continuing
 
Marked Up Text
 
12.5
Contingency Plan
Contingency plan for what to do in case of any part of the care plan failing
If Tony deteriorates the Uptown CMHT  should be contacted to see if further care is needed. This will have to be treated as a new care spell and an MHA Assessment will need doing - if not done already
 
Coded Values:
Snomed CT
110321000000104: mental health contingency plan
 
12.6
Relapse indicators
Observations that may indicate relapse of MH condition
Increased use of Alcohol. Hypomania symptoms.
Irritability
 
Coded Values:
Snomed CT 414482001:
indication for relapse
 
 
End: MH  Care Plan
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
13
Start: MH Risk assessment
For recording Risk Assessment conclusions - as well as supporting information relating to risk

-    Format agreed by consultation with Pan-Cluster Group and wide domain validation
-    Assessment scores will be collected locally and mapped to national representation of categories and scales

N.B. This is a one-way only process for safety
 
 
 
 
13.1
Start: Risk Assessment
This section enables the recording of both the standardised process for capturing risk assessment as well as enabling specific HCR20 Violence Risk Assessments required by Forensic MH Services
 
 
 
Whenever an entry is recorded in this section the person who completed the actual activity must be recorded in section 7.0 (Persons with a connection to the care of this patient) with 7.3.1 (Activity based / Functional or Additional Role) defined as 'Professional completing Risk Assessment'
13.1.1
Date of Risk Assessment
Date when completed
10/09/2006
 
Date
 
13.1.2
Risk Assessment tool used
Defines the tool originally used by the HSCP to complete the risk assessment
Core Face
 
Text
 
13.1.3
Start: MH&SM- SS Risk Assessment Categories and Scale scores
There are up to 14 categories used for the national MH&SM risk recording scale
 
 
 
Only one entry per item in section 13.1.3.1 can exist in a single communication except for 'Other MH&SM Related Risks' which can appear as many times as necessary
13.1.3.1 
Risk Category
What aspect is being assessed against using 14 categories
Violence to Adults,
 
Coded Values:
Suicide,
Self harm,
Accident,
Self neglect,
Vulnerability,
Violence to Adults,
Violence to Children,
Aggression to Adults,
Aggression to Children,
Abuse of Adults,
Abuse of Children,
Neglect of Adults,
Neglect of Children,
Other MH&SM related risks
 
13.1.3.2
Risk scale 
The level of perceived risk over a 5 point semantic scale with an additional item Not_Assessed  (6 items)
Medium
 
Coded Values:
None_Identified,
Low,
Medium,
High,
Extreme,
Not_Assessed,
 
13.1.3.3 
Supporting Risk Information
Text to support above risk conclusion within this category

As well as providing further information about what the 'Other Risk Behaviours' are
Tony becomes verbally abusive when he doesn't get his way. Although he has not actually been physically violent he can be extremely intimidating.
 
Marked Up Text
 
 
End: MH&SM- SS Risk Assessment Categories and Scale scores
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
13.1.4
Start: HCR20 Violence Risk Assessment
Included specifically to meet the requirements of Forensic MH services - but can be used by other service types as well - NB does not include all of the categories of the Standardised Risk Assessment outcome recording described in 13.1.3, so both that section and this one may require completion to obtain a full description of risk issues

Up to 20 Entries per assessment under the 20 items defined within HCR20
 
 
 
Person completing the HCR20 Assessment must always be recorded within section 7.1 'Primary Employed/Professional Role or Relationship Role' with an additional entry under 7.3.1 Activity based / Functional or Additional Role' showing 'Professional completing the HCR20 Violence Risk Assessment',
13.1.4.1
Date of HCR 20 Assessment
Date when this HCR20 Assessment was completed
01/01/2004
 
Date
 
13.1.4.2
Start: HCR20 Violence Risk Assessment Outcome Scores
The following section repeated enables the capture of all 20 of the sub-scale items within the HCR20 Violence Risk Assessment
 
 
 
An Assessment can only allow one entry against each of the items in section 13.1.4.2.1 'HCR20 Sub-Scale & Item' - e.g. there can only be one entry for H1 in this entry
13.1.4.2.1
HCR20 Sub- Scale & Item
Separated into three categories of items:
- Historic (10 items labelled H1 to H10)
- Clinical (5 items labelled C1 to C5)
- Risk Management (5 items labelled R1 to R5)
H1 Previous Violence
 
Coded Values:
H1 Previous Violence
H2 Young Age at First Violent Incident
H3 Relationship Instability
H4 Employment Problems
H5 Substance Use Problems
H6 Major Mental Illness
H7 Psychopathy
H8 Early Maladjustment
H9 Personality Disorder
H10 Prior Supervision Failure
C1 Lack of Insight
C2 Negative Attitudes
C3 Active Symptoms of Major Mental Illness
C4 Impulsivity
C5 Unresponsive to Treatment
R1 Plans Lack Feasibility
R2 Exposure to Destabilizers
R3 Lack of Personal Support
R4 Noncompliance with Remediation Attempts
R5 Stress
 
13.1.4.2.2
HCR20 Score
Scores are 0, 1 or 2 against any item recorded under 13.1.4.2.1 HCR20 Subscale and Item
1
 
Coded Values:
0,
1,
2,
 
13.1.4.2.3
HCR20 Item Further Comment
Enables further comment to describe the issue scored in this item
Indications have recently come to our attention that in his younger life Tony was convicted of Breach of the Peace connected to criminal activities - however there has not been any recent evidence of violent behaviours
 
Marked Up Text
 
 
End: HCR20 Violence Risk Assessment Outcome Scores
 
 
 
 
 
 
End: HCR20 Violence Risk Assessment
 
 
 
 
 
 
End: Risk Assessment
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
13.2
Start: Factors increasing or decreasing risk
 
 
 
 
 
13.2.1 
Increased / Decreased Risk Factor / Issue
Defines the factor or issue that may increase or decrease risk
When he has had a considerable amount of alcohol he can be verbally aggressive
 
Marked Up Text
 
13.2.2 
How this affects Risk
Defines the level of increase or decrease this factor is expected to have
Increases Risk
 
Coded Values:
Reduces risk,
Increases risk,
Extreme risk increase
 
 
End: Factors increasing or decreasing risk
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
13.3
Start: Risk Activities History 
There may be any number of previous events that require recording here and they can be mapped to the standard national categories of MH&SM risk
 
 
 
Only one entry under each of the 14 options for 13.3.1 can be recorded against this persons record
13.3.1
Category for historic Significant Risk Activity
Which of the national standard risk categories this item refers to
Aggression to Adults
 
Coded Values:
Suicide,
Self harm,
Accident,
Self neglect,
Vulnerability,
Violence to Adults,
Violence to Children,
Aggression to Adults,
Aggression to Children,
Abuse of Adults,
Abuse of Children,
Neglect of Adults,
Neglect of Children,
Other MH&SM Risk related Behaviours
 
13.3.2
Risk scale for Historic Risk Activity
The level of perceived risk over a 7 point semantic scale
High
 
Coded Values:
None_Identified,
Low,
Medium,
High,
Extreme,
Not_Assessed,
Insufficient Evidence Recorded to define Risk scale,
 
13.3.3
Details for historic Significant Risk Activity
Text to support the above significant previous risk related event category

As well as providing further information about what the 'Other Risk Behaviours' are
Tony has a long history of aggressive behaviour which whilst it has not seemed to extend into physical violence. He was arrested for a breach of the peace in May 2002 and was bound over to keep the peace at that time
 
Marked Up Text
 
13.3.4
Date for historic Significant Risk Activity
Date of Significant event
01/05/2002
 
Date
 
 
End: Risk Activities History
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
13.4
Start: Vulnerability Details
Details about those identified as having a vulnerability from the patient or from whom the patient may be vulnerable
 
 
 
 
13.4.2
Start: Patient Vulnerabilities
Details of person(s) or group(s) that pose a threat or vulnerability towards the patient from others

NB This information may present significant risk to the person(s) / organisations identified if the patient or others were able to access it - suitable security around options for visibility of this part of the record must be taken into account when enabling it to be rendered to users

NB it is required that 3rd party details are NOT included in messages which will persist on the spine with no ability for Clinician Sealing. However these structures are retained for use in Point-to-Point message applications
 
 
 
NB This information may present significant risk to those identified if the patient or others were able to access it - suitable security around options for visibility of this part of the record must be taken into account when enabling it to be rendered to anyone
13.4.2.1
Person(s), groups or situations from whom the Patient may be vulnerable
Details of the person(s) or groups from whom the patient may be vulnerable
 
Text
 
13.4.2.2
Details of the vulnerability
Further details of the nature of the vulnerability
 
Marked Up Text
 
13.4.2.3
Contact Address for Person(s) or situations creating vulnerability
Contact details of person posing vulnerability towards the patient including: address, phone number(s) and email address (if appropriate)
 
HL7 Unstructured Address
 
13.4.2.4
Contact Telecoms for Person(s) or situations creating vulnerability
Contact details of person posing vulnerability towards the patient including: address, phone number(s) and email address (if appropriate)
 
HL7 Telecoms
 
 
End: Patient Vulnerabilities
 
 
 
 
 
 
End: Vulnerability Details
Details about those who may be placed at a potential risk from the presence of the patient or from whom the patient may be vulnerable
 
 
 
 
 
End: MH Risk Assessment
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
14.0
Start:  Outcome / Measure / Assessment Information
To capture result of Outcome assessments
 
 
 
Whenever an entry is recorded in this section the person who completed the actual activity must be recorded in section 7.0 (Persons with a connection to the care of this patient) with 7.3.1 (Activity based / Functional or Additional Role) defined as 'Professional completing Outcome Measure or Assessment'
14.1
Outcome or Assessment Tool used
Details of the specific Outcome or Assessment tool and set used
HoNOS
 
Coded Values:
HoNOS,
HoNOS 65+,
HoNOS CA,
HoNOS LD,
HoNOS ABI,
HoNOS Secure,
CGAS,
PIC GAS,
SAP,
CAF,
Other
 
14.2
Details of Outcome or Assessment Tool described as 'Other'
Detail of the Tool / set if 'Other' selected previously
 
 
Text
Only for use if 14.1 (Outcome or Assessment Tool used) = 'Other' - otherwise this item is not useable
14.3
Date completed
Date when Outcome or Assessment completed
10/09/2006
 
Date
 
14.4
Start: HoNOS tool outcome scores
 
 
 
 
Can only be used if 14.1 (Outcome or Assessment tool used) = "HoNOS", "HoNOS CA", "HoNOS ABI", "HoNOS +65", "HoNOS LD" or "HoNOS Secure" - otherwise this item not available
14.4.1
Start: HoNOS Score
Standard MH services assessment across a range of aspects of a patients' condition used within CPA. There are 12 HONOS scores (numeric 0, 1, 2, 3, 4) for Adults, HONOS65+, HONOS-LD and HONOS- ABI; 13 for HONOS CA and 7(letters A to G) for HONOS- Secure
 
 
 
Only one inclusion for each HoNOS item at 14.4.1.1 'HoNOS item' should be represented in a completed assessment

e.g. only one entry should be recorded against 'non- accidental self injury'
14.4.1.1
HoNOS item
Gives details of the specific HoNOS item being scored against -  there are 12 specific codes for collecting HONOS Scores
HoNOS item 3 - alcohol/drug problem
 
Text
 
14.4.1.2
HoNOS Score
Detailing the outcome of this assessment aspect - scale 1 to 4 with a 9 = not assessed score
For HoNOS-Secure the 7 scales are coded A to G
3
 
Text
 
 
End: HoNOS Score
 
 
 
 
 
 
End: HoNOS tool outcome scores
 
 
 
 
 
 
End:  Outcome / Measure / Assessment Information
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
15.0
Start: Additional information
The following items cover information for a wide range of additional items to support MH&SM care
 
 
 
 
15.2
MH&SM History
Text item to which HSCPs can add information about the MH&SM History around the care of this individual

Will need to be retrievable and able to be added to over time without loosing the previous contents
No formal mental health history previously

This care episode commenced on 01/01/04, when Tony was admitted with indications of Hypomania under section 2 MHA

After an 8 month admission he was discharged to live independently in the community with support from the CMHT.
He has good insight and is willing to engage with professionals.
 
Marked Up Text
Needs to be able to be added to over time without removing any of the historical content
 
End: Additional information
 
 
 
 
 
MHA ASSESSMENT (OUTSIDE SS)

The trigger for generating a MHA Assessment communication is that a Mental Health Act 1983 assessment has been completed outside of MH&SM-Specialist Services. The MHA Assessment is the formal process through which individuals are assessed for detention in hospital under the Act.

Scenario

Mr Ahmed Khan (Aged 79) was assessed under the Mental Health Act 1983 at his home by Dr Joan Strong Consultant Psychiatrist and his GP.

Mr Khan's wife, Ashum Begum was present. On completion of the assessment Mr Khan is deemed as not detainable and although it would be appropriate to admit him to the local inpatient unit for a thorough assessment, he refused this. He is therefore referred to the local Community Mental Health Team (CMHT) for Older Adults for assessment.

Joan returns to her office and enters the information gathered during the MHA assessment onto her local MH Partnership Trust System which generates an MHA Assessment message for publication to PSIS.  

Level
Name
Item/Group
Definition
MHA ASSESSMENT Scenario Information
MHA ASSESSMENT Scenario Information
Representation
Constraints & Dependencies
1
Patient NHS Number
The currently recorded NHS No for the patient. In the absence of other patient details this enables accurate identification of the patient
12345678
 
UUID
 
2
Start Patient Personal Details
It is important to note that the information in this section has a direct impact upon clinical decision making related to care and risk management beyond its purely administrative usage (hence the need to define further personal information requirements to those within PDS - NB Items delivered by PDS specifications are NOT included in this specification)
 
 
 
 
2.1
Supporting information for use of Aliases or Other Names
Provides supporting reasons to indicate why and in what situations additional names or Aliases are to be / have been used e.g. a child who uses the surname of each parent depending on which one they are staying with at the time
Mr Khan prefers the formal address of 'Mr Khan' rather than for staff to use his first name
 
Marked up Text
 
2.4
Marital or Partnership Status
Information in this section has potentially important clinical implications. The current marital / partnership status of the patient

Unknown at this time is an acceptable answer here
Married
 
Coded Values:
 Married
 Divorced
 Widowed
 Formally Separated
 Informally Separated
 Engaged
 Single
 Formal Civil Partnership
 Informal Same-Sex Partnership
 Informal Opposite-Sex Partnership
 Not Known
 Not Expressed
 
2.5
Start: Housing Status details
(Part of CPA required information collection - Need to know this because of potential risks posed by current housing status - i.e. avoid discharge into a situation that may increase risk or relapse.
 
 
 
 
2.5.1
Housing Status
Describes the patient's housing situation - the nature of the ownership,  payment for or service access to accommodation (not the physical type or relationship living arrangements)

Unknown at this time is an acceptable response here
Owner-occupier
 
Coded Values:
Homeless,
Care_Home,
Probation_Hostel,
Private_Rented, 
LA_rented,
Owner-occupier,
Squatting,
Not Known
 
2.5.2
Start: Housing Type Information
Information about the Physical accommodation type
 
 
 
 
2.5.2.1
Housing Type
Describes the physical accommodation type: 
Bungalow
 
Coded Values:
House,
Bungalow,
Flat,
Mobile home,
Not Known,
Other
 
 
End: Housing Type Information
 
 
 
 
 
2.5.3
Living Arrangements
Further information regarding the living arrangements for this patient

Defines the living arrangement rather than the accommodation circumstances (described elsewhere)
Living with Spouse or Partner
 
Coded Values:
Living on own,
Living in a communal setting,
Living with Friend or Acquaintance,
Adult living in Parental home,
Child living in Parental home,
Child living in Non-Parental home,
Living with Spouse or Partner,
Single-Parent living with child(ren),
Carer living with Dependent,
Not Known
 
 
End: Housing Status details
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
2.6
Start: Ethnicity / Religion/Philosophy/Culture
Ethnicity and personal belief systems can have a significant effect upon how clinical care is conducted - Some cultures are particularly sensitive about MH issues

Unknown is an acceptable response here
 
 
 
 
2.6.1
Ethnic Group
Formal identity of ethnic group
64483007: Indians (Hindi- speaking)
 
Coded Value:
SnomedCT 372148003: ethnic group
 
2.6.2
Religion / Philosophy
Religious or philosophical belief - if expressed
1045 - Shiite (Islam)
 
Coded Values:
HL7 Vocab for Religious Affiliation

PLUS additionally:
Refused
Not Expressed
Not Asked
 
2.6.3
Supporting Ethnicity / Religion / Philosophy / Culture information
Any considerations or requirements affecting care in relation  to religion / philosophy or culture  e.g. Halal food for Muslims or washing requirements linked to religious preferences
Mr Khan is a devout Muslim and he has special dietary requirements e.g. he needs Halal meat, no pork; requires a shower room and prayer facilities.
 
Marked Up Text
 
 
End: Ethnicity / Religion/Philosophy/Culture
 
 
 
 
 
2.8
Start: Education / Training / Employment and Financial status
Information in this section has potentially important clinical implications. Income and employment status can significantly affect the on- going well-being of an individual
 
 
 
 
2.8.1
Employment / Training Status
Needed to ascertain the viability of any work / training being undertaken by the patient. This is a required part of CPA information collection

The patient may have a number of different Jobs

Unknown is an acceptable response here
105493001: retired
 
Coded Values:
SnomedCT 302768007: (employment finding)
& all its children - However NOT Including: 365528005: details relating to place of work and employer - finding
 
2.8.2
Education / Further Education Status
Needed to ascertain the current Educational status or further education being undertaken by this patient at this time.

The patient may be attending a number of different courses or educational establishments

Unknown is an acceptable response here
UNKNOWN
 
Coded Values:
365459005: education currently received - finding
and all its children
ALSO enable
'Undertaking Part-time Education'
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
2.8.5  
Start Financial status Information
 
 
 
 
 
2.8.5.1
Overall financial status
Information about the overall financial situation of the individual

Unknown is an acceptable response here
Currently in receipt of Old Age State Pension as well as a small occupational pension
 
Marked Up Text
 
2.8.5.2
Start: Benefit or Pension details
 
 
 
 
 
2.8.5.2.1
Benefit or Pension information
Details of the current benefits or pensions being received by the patient
State Pension
 
Marked Up Text
 
2.8.5.2.2
Period of benefit / pension validity
The start and end of the current period of validity for pension /benefit 
01/01/2001 -
 
Dates
 
 
End: Benefit or Pension details
 
 
 
 
 
 
End: Financial Status Information
 
 
 
 
 
 
End: Education / Training / Employment and Financial status
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
2.9
Start: Patient Communications and Comprehension
Information in this section has potentially important clinical implications. Often patients with MH problems can find It difficult to communicate for a variety of reasons NB the main language used by the patient is already collected within PDS - this item adds further detail
 
 
 
 
2.9.1
Other languages/forms of communication used
Describes any other spoken languages or formal communication languages used by the patient
UR (Urdu)
 
Coded Value: HL7 vocab HumanLanguage
 
2.9.2
Communication & comprehension Supporting Information
Identifies any perceived support(s) needed relating to ability to communicate e.g. an interpreter for British Sign Language is needed
 
N.B. this is not coded as there is a wide range of observations that may need capturing
Mr Khan's comprehension of English is very good. He will always ask for any documents to be in both English and Urdu. He needs his glasses to read anything
 
Marked Up Text
 
 
End: Patient Communications and Comprehension
 
 
 
 
 
 
End: Patient Personal Details
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
7.0
Start: Person with a connection to the care of this patient
This section includes the details for all persons having a connection to the care of this patient including the reason why they are involved - Professional and Non- Professional, Contracted and Non-contracted, employed, voluntary as well as relatives or friends of the patient  - they are identified as SDS registered or Non-SDS Registered .

The exceptions are those identified already as having a specific role and captured by items within PDS - e.g. Next of Kin

Individuals are described in two ways; firstly In their primary/employed role or Relationship, and secondly in any Activity based, Functional or Additional role at item 7.3.1 (Activity based / Functional or Additional Role)
 
 
 
 
7.1
Date Person Added
The date of the entry of this instance of this person
01/01/2004
01/01/2004
Date
 
7.2
Start: Person, Primary Role and Contact Detail Identifiers
 
 
 
 
 
 
either
 
 
 
 
 
7.2.1
SDS  - Person Identifier
SDS identifier from which local systems can retrieve the persons name, role, contact details and any other information relevant to the employed role they were performing in at the time this message was recorded
(SDS role Identifier)
Null Value
SDS person Identifier
 
 
or
 
 
 
 
 
7.2.2
Start: Non-SDS Person Identifiers
Identifiers and contact details for an individual who does NOT have access to an SDS approved system but requires identification

May be multiple entries
 
 
 
If 7.2.1 SDS Person Identifier - is Null Value (Non SDS) then this item becomes available
7.2.2.1
Non-SDS Person - Name
Name for non-SDS Professional
 
Asha Begum
Text
 
7.2.2.2
Non-SDS Person - Title
Professional Title used  by non-SDS Involved person
 
 
Text
 
7.2.2.3
Non-SDS Person - Organisation
Organisation name for non- SDS Involved Person
 
 
Text
 
7.2.2.4
Non-SDS Person - Department
Department Name for non- SDS Involved Person
 
 
Text
 
7.2.2.5
Non-SDS Person - Address
Address for non-SDS Involved Person
 
14 High Street
Leeds
LS1 1 AA
HL7 Unstructured Address
 
7.2.2.6
Non-SDS Person - Telecoms
Phone Number and e-mail for non-SDS Involved Person
 
0113 000 0000
HL7 Telecoms
 
7.2.2.7
Start: Non-SDS Primary Employed/Professional Role or Relationship Details
 
 
 
 
Only one entry in this section should exist for each individual having an involvement in supporting the care of this patient even if they perform a variety of roles

This section is not for capturing those items described in 7.3.1 (Activity based / Functional or Additional Role)
7.2.2.7.1
Non-SDS Registered Primary / Employed Role or Relationship
The role the person plays as a professional towards this patient
 
Relative or Partner
Coded Values
Probation Officer
Teacher / Trainer,
Foster Carer,
Care Manager,
Support Worker,
Carer (Non-relative),
Relative or Partner,
Friend,
Neighbour,
Work Colleague,
Other
 
7.2.2.7.2
Primary Involvement definition if 'Other'
Defines the Persons Responsibility towards the patient if 'Other'
 
 
Text
If 7.2.2.7.1 Non- SDS Registered Primary / Employed Role or Relationship = 'Other' then this item must be available
7.2.2.7.3
Relative or Partner Relationship
Defines the nature of the relationship if the person is defined as a Relative
 
Wife
Coded Values:
Subset of HL7 PersonRelationshipType Vocab
  Mother
  Father
  Sister
  Brother
  Polygamous partner
  Not Known
  Not specified

Adding the following categories:
  Wife
  Husband
  Person or Organisation with Guardianship Responsibility
  Person or Organisation with Parental Responsibility
  Same-sex Civil Partner
  Informal Partner
  Maternal Grand-Father
  Maternal Grand-Mother
  Paternal Grand-Father
  Paternal Grand-Mother
  Grand-Son
  Grand-Daughter
  Aunt
  Uncle
  Niece
  Nephew
  Step-Mother
  Step-Father
  Step-Son
  Step-Daughter
  Ex-Wife
  Ex-Husband
  Ex-Civil-Partner
If 7.2.2.7.1 Non- SDS Registered Primary / Employed Role or Relationship = 'Relative' then this item must be available
7.2.2.7.4
Friend Relationship
Defines the nature of the relationship if the person is defined as a Friend
 
 
Text
If 7.2.2.7.1 Non- SDS Registered Primary / Employed Role or Relationship = 'Friend' then this item must be available
7.2.2.7.5
Further information describing the way this person supports or delivers care
Provided to give further description if needed of the role this person plays towards someone's care - particularly if obscure or highly specialised
 
Mrs Begum is Mr Khan's wife and primary carer
Marked Up Text
 
7.2.2.7.6
Carer NHS No
Facilitates the recording of the NHS No for a person who may provide direct care to this patient and as a result may require a Carers Assessment
 
09876543
NHS No
If 7.2.2.7.1 Non- SDS Registered Primary / Employed Role or Relationship = either 'Relative', 'Friend', 'Neighbour', 'Work Colleague' or 'Other' then this item must be available, otherwise it must not be available
 
End: Non-SDS Primary Employed/Professional Role or Relationship Details
 
 
 
 
 
 
End: Non-SDS Person Identifiers
 
 
 
 
 
 
End: Person, Primary Role and Contact Detail Identifiers
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
7.3
Start: Activity based, Functional or Additional role of this person towards the patient
 
 
 
 
 
7.3.1
Activity based / Functional or Additional Role
Defines the activity based, functional or additional role this person plays in the care of the patient

e.g. Care Coordinator - they would be firstly a professional in their own right involved in the care and this would be recorded above - OR - a carer (relative or friend) they would first be a relative or friend and additionally recognised as a carer

NB a person may have a number of activity based, functional or additional roles towards a patient

Please note list is longer than screen may represent - ends with 'Other'
Professional completing the Service Assessment
MHA Nearest Relative
Coded Values:
Care Coordinator / Lead Professional/Primary Key Worker,
Proxy Care Coordinator / Lead Professional/Primary Key Worker,
SAP Care Coordinator,
CAF Care Coordinator,
Key Worker,
Named Nurse,
Person making Referral / recommending First Contact
Professional making Triage Decision
Professional completing the Service Assessment
Professional making this decision regarding Entry,
Professional completing this Care Plan component,
Professional making Diagnosis,
Professional completing Risk Assessment,
Professional completing the HCR20 Violence Risk Assessment,
Professional completing the Care Review Summary,
Professional completing Outcome Measure or Assessment,
Professional involved in care review,
Non-Professional involved in review,
Person Authorised to Receive MHA Papers,
MHA Registered Medical Officer,
MHA Section 25 Supervisor,
MHA Legal Guardian,
Person requesting MHA Assessment,
Doctor making MHA Recommendation 1,
Doctor making MHA Recommendation 2,
ASW making application for detention under MHA,
Nearest Relative making application for detention under MHA,
MHA Nearest Relative,
Person holding a Power of Attorney,
Person Acting with Parental Responsibility,
Non-MHA Guardian,
Home Help,
Volunteer,
Nominated / Preferred Contact,
Carer,
Other
 
7.3.2
Date Added
The date of this entry of this instance describing the functional role of this person
01/01/2004
01/01/2004
Date
 
7.3.3
Date Applicable Until or Ended
The End of this role or when it  is expected that it will no longer be applicable
01/01/2004 
Date
 
7.3.4
Details for 'Other'
Defines the functional or secondary role this person plays in the care of the patient if described at 7.3.1 as 'Other'
 
 
Text
Only for use if item 7.3.1 (Activity based / Functional or Additional Role) ='Other'
7.3.5
MHA Nearest Relative - relationship
This is clinically relevant as it Identifies the type of relationship the Nearest Relative has with the Patient as there is a specific hierarchy within the Act as to who can take on this role -
/i.      Husband or Wife
ii.      Son or Daughter
iii.      Father or Mother
iv.      Brother or Sister
v.      Grandparent
vi.      Grandchild
vii.      Uncle or Aunt
viii.      Nephew or Niece
ix.      Someone, not being a relative, with whom the person concerned normally resides, and has done so for at least 5 years
 
Wife
Coded Values:  
Husband
Wife
Son
Daughter
Father
Mother
Brother
Sister
Grandparent
Grandchild
Uncle
Aunt
Nephew
Niece
Not a Relative but a person lived with for at least 5 years
If 7.3.1 (Activity based / Functional or Additional Role) = 'MHA Nearest Relative'
Then this item must be available. Otherwise this item is not available.
 
End: Activity based, Functional or Additional role of this person towards the patient
 
 
 
 
 
 
End: Person with a connection to the care of this patient
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
9.0
Start: Diagnosis/es and Registered Disabilities/Other Conditions
Diagnosis/es to be recorded PSIS
 
 
 
 
9.1
Start: Diagnosis Coding
Snomed CT and ICD-10 (but only where a mapped Snomed CT Code exists)codes for diagnoses
 
 
 
Whenever an entry is recorded in this section the person who completed the actual activity must be recorded in section 7.0 (Persons with a connection to the care of this patient) with 7.3.1 (Activity based / Functional or Additional Role) defined as 'Professional making Diagnosis'
9.1.1
Diagnosis 
Diagnostic coding - completed by HSCP using longhand descriptor to generate code - receiving system will render longhand diagnosis from code
Depression
 
Coded Values:
Snomed CT

41006004
 
9.1.2
Mental Health ICD-10 Representation
ICD-10 is a list of codes widely used for diagnostic purposes within MH&SM-SSs - Can exist as coded item as long as a Snomed code with a direct relationship to the ICD-10 code used is also present 
F32
 
Coded Values:
ICD-10
An ICD-10 code here would be generated only following an equivalent Snomed CT code being entered at 9.1.1 (Diagnosis) i.e. This item can only be included if it has a directly mapped relationship to 9.1.1 (Diagnosis)
9.1.3
Date of Diagnosis 
Date the Diagnosis was formally recorded
01/01/2004
 
Date
 
 
End: Diagnosis Coding
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
9.2
Start: Registered Disability / Other condition(s)
Conditions or Disabilities that may or may not currently be formally captured as diagnosis/es - these will usually form the basis of observations by HSCPs and could include minor conditions for which the patient is self-treating as well as treatments or supports needed to enable the patient to be cared for
 
 
 
 
9.2.1
Disability or condition not otherwise mentioned
These will usually form the basis of observations by HSCPs as well as registered Disabilities for which there may or may not be current treatment underway
Mr Khan is considerably overweight for his age and height
 
Text
 
9.2.2
Care / Treatment or Support required
Description of the care, Treatment or support needed for this Condition
 Consider referral to a dietician
 
Marked Up Text
 
9.2.3
Is this a Registered Disability?
Defines if this item is a disability for which the patient is formally Registered as Disabled
No
 
Boolean:
Yes/No
 
 
End: Registered Disability / Other condition(s)
 
 
 
 
 
 
End: Diagnosis/es and Registered Disabilities/Other Conditions
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
11.0
Start: Legal Activities 
 
 
 
 
 
11.1
Start: Mental Health Act 1983 Assessment
 
 
 
 
Whenever an entry is recorded in this section the person(s) involved in the recommendation(s) and approval of the actual MHA Assessment must be recorded in section 7.0 (Persons with a connection to the care of this patient) with an additional entry/ies under 7.3.1 (Activity based / Functional or Additional Role) defined as the appropriate combination of: 'Doctor making MHA Assessment recommendation 1', 'Doctor making MHA Assessment recommendation 2', 'ASW approving MHA Assessment' or 'MHA Nearest Relative',
11.1.1
Date and time of MHA Assessment
When MHA Assessment completed
01/01/04: 09:15
 
Date and Time
 
11.1.2
Location where MHA Assessment completed
Defines the type of location where the MHA Assessment completed e.g. Patient's home, Police Station, Acute Ward, ED (A&E) etc.
Patient's Home
 
Text
 
11.1.3
Reason for MHA Assessment
Gives an outline of the situation that lead up to an MHA Assessment being required
Patient appeared to be in a delusional and confused state
 
Marked Up Text
 
11.1.4
Outcome of Assessment
Gives the outcome of the joint agreed decision of the MHA Assessment including recommendations for future care
Patient not deemed detainable under the MHA. Refused inpatient care.
Patient to be referred to CMHT
 
Marked Up Text
 
11.1.5
When MHA Papers received by authorised professional
Date / Time that Papers logged with authorised person
NA
 
Date and Time
Whenever an entry is recorded for this item the person who completed the actual activity must be recorded in section 7.0 (Persons with a connection to the care of this patient) with 7.3.1 (Activity based / Functional or Additional Role) defined as 'Person Authorised to Receive MHA Papers'
11.1.6
Organisation SDS code of unit to which patient is detained
Organisation code for MHA Administrators
NA
 
Coded:
SDS Organisation Identifier
 
11.1.7
End of period within which MHA Papers may be served 
End of the period where papers authorised for enacting a section may be served
NA
 
Date and Time
 
 
End: Mental Health Act 1983 Assessment
 
 
 
 
 
11.2
Is Patient currently subject to MHA '83?
Checks whether the patient is subject to MHA '83 (Excluding MHA Section 117)
No
 
Boolean
(Yes/No)
Excludes MHA section 117

A “Yes” answer may only be given when the MHA papers have been accepted by the person authorised to receive them
 
End: Mental Health Act 1983 Activities
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
11.4
